text.textcontent: ICAO and the global aviation community adopted the World Geodetic System of 1984 (WGS-84), and set January 1998 as the compliance date. Many countries have complied, but some have not; so it is very important when navigating internationally using any satellite-based system for crews to know if a country is compliant.
If not compliant, use only ground-based navigation systems in that country.

Jeppesen publishes a WGS-84 Status Report where you may check on the status of countries worldwide. It is the pilot's responsibility to verify compliance before relying on satellite-based navigation in international airspace.

The WGS-84 system provides a standard coordinate reference frame for the Earth, used for GPS and other satellite navigation systems. Without WGS-84 compliance, there may be discrepancies between charted positions and actual GPS positions.
